name: Eekso — The Pitfall Prison
dmNotes:
THE HORROR FLOOR. Everything here is one idea expressed four ways: the necromancer HARVESTS, and the building is plumbing. Slanted floors, gutters, pipes — all of it runs downhill toward Level 2. Once the party notices the floors are tilted, every room gets worse retroactively. That's the whole trick; set it up early.

Two entrances: dropped through a rug (start at 'The Landing') or walked down the stairwell (start at 'The Prison'). If the party is split, run the dropped PC alone for a few minutes — this floor is much worse by yourself.

TONE: not a gross-out. It's cold and industrial. The necromancer isn't gleeful, he's TIDY. Nothing here is wasted. That's what should disturb them.

The pawn is a recording, not a person — see his section. Play him with zero affect.
scriptText:
# The Pitfall Prison

> [!dm] **Start here only if someone fell through a rug.** If they walked down the stairwell, skip to *The Prison*.

## The Landing

{{music:landing}} You come down thirty feet in the dark and you land on points.

> [!dm] **3d6 bludgeoning + 2d10 piercing** (DC 13 Dex halves the spike damage only).

The spikes are iron, set upright in a bed of them, and they are not new — they're pitted and dark and every one of them is stained most of the way up.

When you move, you hear the sound the floor makes. Not stone. Something wetter than stone.

And then, from somewhere off in the dark that you cannot see the end of, you hear the *drip* — but it isn't water, it's heavier than water, and it's landing in something that already has some in it.

> [!dm] Alone in the dark, hurt, on a bed of used spikes. Sit in it. Ask them what they do before you give them anything else.
>
> If the rest of the party is above, cut away and leave them there a beat.

## The Prison

{{music:dread}} The stair bottoms out into a corridor of cells — iron bars set into old stone, most standing open, hinges rusted through.

It was a prison once, honestly. Before it was anything else.

**West side:** the cells hold bones. Old ones. Sitting where people sat down and stopped — irons still around wrist-bones, jaws open. Nothing about these is recent; whatever happened here happened lifetimes ago and stopped.

**East side** is different. The east cells have bodies in them, and the bodies are *fresh enough to still be people*.

- Three, four of them. Slumped, grey, mouths open
- No wounds. No violence. They look drained — because they are
- The floor of each cell slopes gently toward a gutter cut along the back wall

> [!dm] **The floors are tilted.** Plant this now, quietly, and never explain it. Once they see it here they will see it everywhere on this floor, and by the time they reach the pipes they'll have worked it out themselves.

### The Pawn

In the last cell on the east side, one of them lifts his head.

> [!dm] He is alive in the strictest possible sense and he is not in there. Flat voice, no emphasis, eyes not quite tracking. He says his piece, and if they ask questions he **says it again**, the same way, from the top.

He says:

**"Don't trust what you see in there. None of it is real. None of it is real. You can walk right through. It's all just paint and old bones and he wants you frightened — that's all he has. Don't trust what you see. You can walk right through."**

> [!dm] It is a lie, planted to walk them into the Art Room without their guard up.
>
> - **Insight DC 13:** he's not lying — he isn't *present* enough to lie. He's reciting.
> - **Insight DC 16 / Arcana DC 15:** the words aren't his. Something is speaking through the shape of him.
> - **Medicine DC 12:** he should be dead. He has been kept, deliberately, at the edge.
> - If they try to free him: he'll walk where they push him and say the line again. If they get him out, he dies within the hour, peacefully, mid-sentence.
> - If they ask his name, there's a long pause — the only crack in it — and then he starts the line again from the beginning.

### Cell Traps

> [!dm] Two or three, DM's choice of placement — the cells that still hold anything worth taking:
> - **Dart trap** in a cell door frame: Perception DC 14, +6 to hit, 2d4 piercing + 2d6 poison (DC 13 Con half)
> - **Spike trap** under loose flagstones: Investigation DC 15, DC 14 Dex or 2d10 piercing
> - **Glyph of warding** scratched under a bunk, faded but live: Investigation DC 16, DC 15 Dex, 4d8 necrotic
>
> These are old, from an earlier owner. The necromancer didn't set them. He just never bothered to clear them.

## The Plumbing

{{music:walk}} As they move deeper, the building's real design becomes obvious.

Every floor on this level is pitched — a slow, deliberate slope, a few degrees, always the same direction. Where floors meet walls there are gutters. Where gutters meet corners there are grates. And out of the grates run pipes: clay and iron, strapped along the walls at knee height, all of them heading the same way.

Downhill. Downstairs. Toward Level 2.

They are not empty. You can hear them.

> [!dm] The horror is the *engineering*, not the gore. Somebody surveyed this floor. Somebody worked out the fall required. Somebody laid pipe.
>
> - **Investigation DC 10:** every slope, every gutter, every pipe runs to the same place
> - The pipes are warm to the touch
> - This is not a river system. Nothing here connects to the water outside. This is a **closed circuit** that somebody built by hand.

## The Stacks

{{music:stacks}} {{amb:ghostly-loop|vol=25%|in=6s|until=section}} The southwest of the floor is a storeroom, and it has been filled the way a storeroom gets filled.

Bodies. Floor to ceiling, wall to wall, stacked in rows with walkways left between them.

The ones at the bottom are old — dry, collapsed, more or less bone. Higher up they get fresher. Near the top, under grey blankets pulled up neat, are people who look like they were alive last week.

Each one has a tube in the arm. Each tube runs to a line along the floor. Each line runs to the gutter, and the gutter runs downhill.

And some of them are still breathing.

> [!dm] **The worst room in the fortress. Read it slowly and then stop talking.**
>
> The ones still breathing are being kept — not healed, not tortured, just *kept*, because fresh is better than not fresh.
>
> - **Medicine DC 12:** they're deeply unconscious, unwakeable, and they are not going to recover
> - **Arcana DC 15:** they're held on the exact line between alive and not by something continuous and deliberate. Kill the caster and they go.
> - Somebody has tucked the blankets in. Somebody straightened them. That detail does more work than any amount of blood.
>
> **If a player tries to save them:** be honest and be kind. They can end it here, one at a time, by hand — and every player at the table will remember who volunteered. Don't punish it. Don't rush it.

## The Grinding Room

{{amb:nox-old-mill|vol=35%|in=4s|until=section}} Off the stacks, a smaller room, and the sound coming out of it is machinery — a slow, patient, turning sound.

A hand-cranked mill sits at the middle of it, the kind a village uses for grain, and it has not been used for grain. The hopper is full. The stone is dark. Under the spout, sacks stand in a tidy row, filled and folded shut, stacked against the wall by size.

What's in them is bone. Ground fine, and pale, and dry.

- The sacks are labelled. Not with names — with **dates**
- A ledger hangs on a nail: tally marks, weights, and a running total
- The crank is worn smooth and shiny where a hand goes

> [!dm] The sacks go downstairs. They are an *ingredient*. When the party reaches the ritual chamber and sees what the Torm symbol is painted with, this room should snap into place for them.
>
> {{music:grief}} If anyone reads the ledger closely: the totals are in a neat hand, and the columns balance. Nothing has been wasted.

## Onward — The Blocked Corridor

East of the spike-pit, a corridor runs toward the far stair — and it's shut. A section of ceiling has come down and filled it wall to wall with rubble.

- **Investigation DC 12:** the collapse is old, and it was never cleared. There's no way through in any reasonable time.
- The only other way on is a doorway to the south, and past it the darkness looks strange — it isn't flat. Something in it is *hanging*, and it moves slightly, and it makes a small dry sound.

> [!dm] This is the funnel. The rubble exists to make the Art Room the only door.
>
> They've just been told by a dying man that none of it is real. Switch to **'Eekso — The Art Room'**.

ideas.text: Every floor on this level is pitched a few degrees the same direction. Plant it in the cells, never explain it.
The pipes are warm to the touch
Someone tucked the blankets in around the fresh bodies and straightened them
The bonemeal sacks are labelled with dates, not names, and the ledger's columns balance
Ask the pawn his name and there's a long pause — the only crack — then he restarts the line
Nothing here connects to the river. It's a closed circuit somebody built by hand.

entities.name: The pawn (last east cell)
entities.note: A recording, not a person. 'None of it is real — you can walk right through.' A lie to lower their guard for the Art Room. Dies within the hour if freed.

entities.name: The stacks
entities.note: Bodies floor to ceiling, tubed and drained. Some still breathing, kept deliberately.

entities.name: The grinding room
entities.note: Hand mill, bonemeal in dated sacks, a balanced ledger. The sacks are an ingredient for downstairs.

entities.name: The plumbing
entities.note: Pitched floors, gutters, warm pipes. All of it runs downhill to Level 2.

entities.name: Collapsed corridor
entities.note: Funnels the party into the Art Room — the only way on.
